user.title: Connect Emby server
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to your local Emby server. The API key is stored in Home Assistant and redacted from diagnostics.

reconfigure.title: Edit connection
reconfigure.description: Change the server address, port, HTTPS setting or API key. Leave the API-key field empty to keep the stored key.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.

abort.already_configured: This Emby server is already configured.
abort.unique_id_mismatch: The new connection belongs to another Emby server.

init.title: Manage EMBi
init.description:
Manage Home Assistant players and clean up old Emby server records in clearly separated areas.

{server_history} records in Emby server history
{ha_players} registered Home Assistant players
{protected} playing or paused
{server_missing} no longer reported by the Emby server
Automatic cleanup: {automatic_cleanup}

ha_players.title: Home Assistant players
ha_players.description:
Global visibility settings are shown first. Open a short user or client group to manage its master setting, exceptions or technical details.

{ha_players} registered players
{playing} playing or paused
{known_users} recognized users
{server_missing} no longer reported by the Emby server

data.search_query: Search user, device, app or entity ID

data_description.only_during_playback: When enabled, players appear only while playing or paused.
data_description.auto_show_new_players: When disabled, current visible players remain allowed and new players stay hidden until selected.
data_description.technical_access_visibility: Only explicitly technical or API-only clients are controlled by this setting.
data_description.selected_group: Groups use short names and a player count.
data_description.player_action: Remove or restore Home Assistant players in this player area.
data_description.back: Return without closing the complete dialog. The current draft is preserved.

player_group.title: {group}
player_group.description: {count} players are assigned to this group. User groups provide one master switch. Individual players are changed only under Manage exceptions.

data.show_user_players: Show all players for this user

player_exceptions.title: Manage exceptions
player_exceptions.description:
Only the current page is changed. Selector labels contain only device, app and—when needed for distinction—the user.

{count} matching players · page {page} of {pages}

player_details.title: Player details
player_details.description:
Technical details are shown only on this dedicated page.

{details}

automatic_cleanup.title: Automatic cleanup
automatic_cleanup.description: Configure the automatic server-history cleanup. These changes remain in the draft until Review changes is applied.

data.server_auto_cleanup_remove_ha_entities: Also remove the related Home Assistant player after successful server deletion

server_history_check.title: Check for old Emby records now
server_history_check.description:
{total} records checked
{candidates} older than {age_days} days and eligible
{active} protected by active playback
{recent} too recent
{without_activity} without a valid activity timestamp

Selecting records is the preview. The next page contains exactly one clearly named execution action.

confirm_server_deletion.title: Delete {count} Emby records?
confirm_server_deletion.description: Candidates are refreshed and validated again immediately before execution. Users, media, libraries and playback history are not deleted.

last_cleanup_run.title: Last cleanup run

manage_ha_players.title: Remove Home Assistant players
manage_ha_players.description:
{ha_players} registered players
{removable} currently removable
{protected} protected by playback or an unresolved state
{server_missing} no longer reported by the Emby server
Page {page} of {pages}

Selecting players is the preview. The next page contains exactly one removal action.

confirm_ha_removal.title: Remove {count} Home Assistant players?
confirm_ha_removal.description: The selected players are hidden in EMBi and removed from the registry only after fresh ownership, platform, config-entry, unique-ID and playback validation.

restore_ha_players.title: Restore Home Assistant players
restore_ha_players.description: {candidates} hidden players can be restored · page {page} of {pages}. Restoration removes only the exact hidden rule and verifies the entity after reload.

review_changes.title: Review changes

older_rules.title: Older rules
older_rules.description: {count} older rules could not be assigned safely during migration. They remain preserved and inactive until reviewed.

error.cannot_connect: Current Emby and Home Assistant data could not be refreshed. Try again or go back.
error.playback_protected: Stop playback before hiding or removing this player.
error.invalid_age: Choose a preset or enter a valid custom number of days.
error.invalid_selection: The selected target changed or is no longer safe. Refresh the page and try again.
error.unsaved_changes: Apply or discard the current draft before starting a destructive action.

abort.apply_complete: Changes applied. Restored: {restored}. Enabled: {enabled}. Protected: {protected}. Failed: {failed}.
abort.ha_player_removal_complete: Home Assistant player removal completed. Requested: {requested}. Removed: {removed}. Protected: {protected}. Failed: {failed}.
abort.ha_player_restore_complete: Home Assistant player restoration completed. Requested: {requested}. Restored: {restored}. Failed: {failed}.
